overview: The leave-service has no dependencies and forms the foundation of the system, so it is built first. The leave-portal depends on leave-service for all API calls and cannot be meaningfully developed or tested without it, so it follows in Phase 2. This bottom-up ordering ensures each layer is ready before the layer above it is assembled.

phases.name: Foundation Services
phases.description: The leave-service is the sole backend and has no dependencies on other components. It must be implemented first as it owns all business logic, data storage, and API contracts that the frontend will consume.

components.name: leave-service
responsibilities: Handle submission, retrieval, and status updates of employee leave requests
Enforce business rules including date-range validation, overlap detection, and balance sufficiency checks
Manage leave balances per employee and deduct approved leave from the appropriate balance
Support role-based filtering of leave requests for employees, managers, and HR admins
Expose a /health endpoint for Kubernetes readiness probes
components.architectureContext: This is the sole backend service in the system. It is called directly by the leave-portal for all data operations. It stores all state in-memory (maps with mutexes) and seeds initial employee, manager, and balance data at startup. Nothing depends on an external service — it is the system's source of truth.
keyConsiderations: In-memory store must use sync.RWMutex correctly to avoid data races under concurrent requests — use read locks for GET operations and write locks for POST/PATCH/PUT.
Balance deduction must only occur when a request transitions to 'approved' via PATCH, not at submission time; guard against double-deduction if PATCH is called more than once.
Overlap detection must check all existing non-denied requests for the same employeeId where date ranges intersect before creating a new request.
Manager authorization in PATCH must verify that the reviewerId is registered as the direct manager of the leave request's employeeId — return 403 otherwise.
Multi-stage Dockerfile must produce a minimal scratch-based final image; ensure the binary is built with CGO_ENABLED=0 and GOOS=linux for compatibility with the scratch base.
components.apiContract:
openapi: 3.0.3
info:
  title: Leave Management API
  version: 1.0.0
  description: Backend API for WSO2 internal leave management system
servers:
  - url: http://localhost:9090
    description: Development server
paths:
  /health:
    get:
      summary: Health check endpoint
      operationId: healthCheck
      responses:
        '200':
          description: Service is healthy
          content:
            application/json:
              schema:
                type: object
                properties:
                  status:
                    type: string
                    example: ok
  /api/v1/leave-requests:
    post:
      summary: Submit a new leave request
      operationId: createLeaveRequest
      requestBody:
        required: true
        content:
          application/json:
            schema:
              type: object
              required:
                - employeeId
                - leaveType
                - startDate
                - endDate
              properties:
                employeeId:
                  type: string
                leaveType:
                  type: string
                  enum: [annual, sick, personal, unpaid]
                startDate:
                  type: string
                  format: date
                endDate:
                  type: string
                  format: date
                reason:
                  type: string
      responses:
        '201':
          description: Leave request created
          content:
            application/json:
              schema:
                $ref: '#/components/schemas/LeaveRequest'
        '400':
          description: Invalid request (overlapping dates, insufficient balance)
        '500':
          description: Server error
    get:
      summary: List leave requests
      operationId: listLeaveRequests
      parameters:
        - name: employeeId
          in: query
          schema:
            type: string
        - name: managerId
          in: query
          schema:
            type: string
        - name: status
          in: query
          schema:
            type: string
            enum: [pending, approved, denied]
      responses:
        '200':
          description: List of leave requests
          content:
            application/json:
              schema:
                type: array
                items:
                  $ref: '#/components/schemas/LeaveRequest'
  /api/v1/leave-requests/{id}:
    get:
      summary: Get a specific leave request
      operationId: getLeaveRequest
      parameters:
        - name: id
          in: path
          required: true
          schema:
            type: string
      responses:
        '200':
          description: Leave request details
          content:
            application/json:
              schema:
                $ref: '#/components/schemas/LeaveRequest'
        '404':
          description: Leave request not found
    patch:
      summary: Update leave request status (approve/deny)
      operationId: updateLeaveRequestStatus
      parameters:
        - name: id
          in: path
          required: true
          schema:
            type: string
      requestBody:
        required: true
        content:
          application/json:
            schema:
              type: object
              required:
                - status
                - reviewerId
              properties:
                status:
                  type: string
                  enum: [approved, denied]
                reviewerId:
                  type: string
                comments:
                  type: string
      responses:
        '200':
          description: Leave request updated
          content:
            application/json:
              schema:
                $ref: '#/components/schemas/LeaveRequest'
        '403':
          description: Unauthorized (not the manager)
        '404':
          description: Leave request not found
  /api/v1/employees/{id}/balance:
    get:
      summary: Get employee leave balance
      operationId: getLeaveBalance
      parameters:
        - name: id
          in: path
          required: true
          schema:
            type: string
      responses:
        '200':
          description: Leave balance by type
          content:
            application/json:
              schema:
                type: object
                properties:
                  employeeId:
                    type: string
                  balances:
                    type: array
                    items:
                      type: object
                      properties:
                        leaveType:
                          type: string
                        allocated:
                          type: number
                        used:
                          type: number
                        remaining:
                          type: number
        '404':
          description: Employee not found
    put:
      summary: Set employee annual leave allocation (HR admin only)
      operationId: setLeaveAllocation
      parameters:
        - name: id
          in: path
          required: true
          schema:
            type: string
      requestBody:
        required: true
        content:
          application/json:
            schema:
              type: object
              required:
                - leaveType
                - allocated
              properties:
                leaveType:
                  type: string
                allocated:
                  type: number
      responses:
        '200':
          description: Allocation updated
        '403':
          description: Unauthorized (not HR admin)
components:
  schemas:
    LeaveRequest:
      type: object
      properties:
        id:
          type: string
        employeeId:
          type: string
        leaveType:
          type: string
        startDate:
          type: string
          format: date
        endDate:
          type: string
          format: date
        reason:
          type: string
        status:
          type: string
          enum: [pending, approved, denied]
        reviewerId:
          type: string
        reviewerComments:
          type: string
        createdAt:
          type: string
          format: date-time
        updatedAt:
          type: string
          format: date-time

phases.name: Frontend Application
phases.description: The leave-portal depends entirely on leave-service for its data. It is implemented after the backend is stable so API calls can be validated end-to-end during development.

components.name: leave-portal
responsibilities: Render an Employee Dashboard showing leave balances and submitted requests, with a form to submit new leave requests
Render a Manager Dashboard listing pending requests from direct reports with Approve/Deny actions
Render an HR Admin Panel allowing leave allocation updates per employee
Route between views based on the user role stored in localStorage and proxy all data operations to leave-service
Serve the compiled React SPA as static files via an Express server that also exposes a /health endpoint
components.architectureContext: The leave-portal is the only client-facing component. It communicates exclusively with leave-service (at http://leave-service:9090) via the browser's fetch API for all CRUD operations. It has no backend logic of its own — all validation and business rules are enforced by leave-service. It is accessed by end users on port 3000.
keyConsiderations: The hard-coded userId and role in localStorage is a demo-only pattern; ensure the UI reads these values on every route render so switching roles in localStorage is reflected immediately without a full page reload.

All fetch calls to leave-service must handle non-2xx responses explicitly and surface human-readable error messages (e.g. 'Overlapping leave request exists', 'Insufficient balance') extracted from the response body.
The Express server.js must serve the Vite /dist output as static files and fall back to index.html for all non-/health routes to support React Router's client-side navigation.
The Dockerfile's builder stage must run `npm run build` against the correct Vite config; the runtime stage should only copy dist/ and server.js to keep the final image lean.
Date pickers in the leave submission form must enforce that endDate >= startDate on the client side to provide fast feedback before the request is sent to leave-service.
components.apiContract:
openapi: 3.0.3
info:
  title: Leave Portal Frontend
  version: 1.0.0
  description: Static web application serving the leave management UI
servers:
  - url: http://localhost:3000
paths:
  /health:
    get:
      summary: Health check
      responses:
        '200':
          description: Frontend is serving
          content:
            application/json:
              schema:
                type: object
                properties:
                  status:
                    type: string
                    example: ok
